B.x: A complete reference showcasing all 20 block types with realistic, production-quality content
The Heading Block Anchors Every Slide

The text block handles body copy with configurable size, color, and weight. Use it for supporting detail, captions, or descriptive prose that elaborates on your headline assertion. It pairs naturally with headings and dividers to build a clear visual hierarchy.
Design is not just what it looks like and feels like. Design is how it works.
B.author: Steve Jobs

B.x: Always validate user input before processing — never trust data from external sources without sanitization. XSS, SQL injection, and SSRF are the top three vectors for web application compromise.

S.n: Steps Turn Complexity Into a Repeatable Recipe

I.title: Install the CLI
I.x: Run npm install -g @vela/cli and verify with vela --version in your terminal

I.title: Initialize your deck
I.x: Run vela init my-deck to scaffold a new .vela file with sensible defaults

I.title: Author in DSL
I.x: Edit the compact JSON — the engine validates block types and colors in real time

I.title: Preview locally
I.x: Run vela server start my-deck.vela to hot-reload at localhost:3030

I.title: Ship to cloud
I.x: Run vela deck ship my-deck.vela --output deck.jsx to publish a shareable artifact

S.n: Spacer & Divider Give Layouts Room to Breathe

B.x: A spacer block (numeric shorthand 12) inserts configurable vertical whitespace between any two blocks — essential for breathing room in dense slides.
The subtle divider above uses color $D. It structures content without adding visual noise — ideal for separating related groups within a single slide.
An accent-colored divider commands attention. Use it to signal a major context shift or to bookend a critical data point before your conclusion.

S.n: Every Block Type Is Now in Your Toolkit

B.x: Mix, match, and compose these blocks to build any presentation — from investor decks to API documentation to internal engineering reviews.
B.title: Build Anything
